# terrible_openstack_cloud_port (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name that has to be given to the port. This port attribute cannot be updated.

### Optional

- `allowed_address_pairs` (String) Allowed address pairs list. Allowed address pairs are supported with dictionary structure. e.g.  allowed_address_pairs: - ip_address: 10.1.0.12 mac_address: ab:cd:ef:12:34:56 - ip_address: ... The port will change during update if not all suboptions are specified, e.g. when ip_address is given but mac_address is not.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `binding_profile` (String) Binding profile dict that the port should be created with.
- `binding_vnic_type` (String) The type of the port that should be created
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description of the port.
- `device_id` (String) Device ID of device using this port.
- `device_owner` (String) The ID of the entity that uses this port.
- `dns_domain` (String) The dns domain of the port ( only with dns-integration enabled )
- `dns_name` (String) The dns name of the port ( only with dns-integration enabled )
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name竊致alue).
- `extra_dhcp_opts` (String) Extra dhcp options to be assigned to this port. Extra options are supported with dictionary structure. Note that options cannot be removed only updated. e.g.  extra_dhcp_opts: - ip_version: 4 opt_name: bootfile-name opt_value: pxelinux.0 - opt_name: ... The port will change during update if not all suboptions are specified, e.g. when opt_name is given but ip_version is not.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fixed_ips` (String) Desired IP and/or subnet for this port.  Subnet is referenced by subnet_id and IP is referenced by ip_address. The port will change during update if not all suboptions are specified, e.g. when ip_address is given but subnet_id is not.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `is_admin_state_up` (Boolean) Sets admin state.
- `is_port_security_enabled` (Boolean) Whether to enable or disable the port security on the network.
- `mac_address` (String) MAC address of this port.
- `network` (String) ID or name of the network this port belongs to. Required when creating a new port. Must be a name when creating a port. This port attribute cannot be updated.
- `no_security_groups` (Boolean) Do not associate a security group with this port. Deprecated. Use *security_groups*: `[]` instead of *no_security_groups*: `true`.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `security_groups` (String) Security group(s) ID(s) or name(s) associated with the port.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Should the resource be present or absent.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `port` (String) Dictionary describing the port.
